judgment.summary: High-margin, cash-rich business facing decelerating revenue growth and negative near-term analyst projections.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS was 1.44 vs an estimate of 1.49 (miss), with the next earnings date scheduled for 2026-05-21.
positives: Profit margin 39.0% — demonstrates strong bottom-line efficiency.
Total cash $7.82B vs $58.48M debt — represents an exceptionally strong balance sheet.
Free cash flow $1.96B (TTM) — shows robust cash generation.
Gross margin 77.4% — reflects high-quality service margins.
negatives: Revenue growth 5.3% — indicates slowing top-line momentum.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year are both negative.
PEG ratio 4.21 — suggests valuation is high relative to growth.
Mean analyst target $97.93 — sits below the current spot price of $100.16.

news.paragraph: Zoom is facing conflicting signals as recent headlines report shares plummeting and falling more steeply than the broader market, alongside a 100% reduction in an insider stake. Conversely, Citigroup recently raised its price target for the stock to $122 from $106 while maintaining a buy rating. Options traders should weigh this recent downward price momentum against the improved analyst outlook.
